15.14: “Why? asked Pilate (armed with a spear). “What evil has He done? But they shouted all the louder, “Crucify Him!
15.15: And wishing to satisfy the crowd, Pilate (armed with a spear) released Barabbas (Son of Abba) to them. But he had Jesus (YHWH saves) flogged, and handed Him over to be crucified.
15.16: Then the soldiers led Jesus away into the palace (that is, the Praetorium) and called the whole company together.
15.17: They dressed Him in a purple robe, twisted together a crown of thorns, and set it on His head.
15.18: And they began to salute Him: “Hail, King of the Jews (people of praise)!
15.19: They kept striking His head with a staff and spitting on Him. And they knelt down and bowed before Him.
15.20: After they had mocked Him, they removed the purple robe and put His own clothes back on Him. Then they led Him out to crucify Him.
15.21: Now Simon (hearing) of Cyrene, the father {patera} of Alexander (defending men) and Rufus (red-haired), was passing by on his way in from the country, and the soldiers forced him to carry the cross of Jesus.
15.22: They brought Jesus to a place called Golgotha, which means The Place of the Skull.
15.23: There they offered Him wine mixed with myrrh, but He did not take it.
15.24: And they crucified Him. They also divided His garments by casting lots to decide what each of them would take .
15.25: It was the third hour (9:00 AM) when they crucified Him.
15.26: And the charge inscribed against Him read: THE KING OF THE JEWS (people of praise).
15.27: Along with Jesus, they crucified two robbers (insurrectionists), one on His right and one on His left. [28 So the Scripture was fulfilled that says, “And He was numbered with the transgressors.”]
15.29: And those who passed by heaped abuse on Him, shaking their heads and saying, “Aha! You who are going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days,
15.30: come down from the cross and save Yourself!
15.31: In the same way, the chief priests and scribes mocked Him among themselves, saying, “He saved others, but He cannot save Himself!
15.32: Let the Christ (the Anointed One), the King of Israel (He wrestles with God), come down now from the cross, so that we may see and believe! And even those who were crucified with Him berated Him.
15.33: From the sixth hour until the ninth hour (from noon until 3 pm) darkness came over all the land.
15.34: At the ninth hour, Jesus (YHWH saves) cried out in a loud voice, “Eloi, Eloi, lema sabachthani? which means, “My God {Theos}, My God, why have You forsaken Me ?”
15.35: When some of those standing nearby heard this, they said, “Behold (behold), He is calling Elijah (my God is YHWH).
15.36: And someone ran and filled a sponge with sour wine (wine vinegar). He put it on a reed and held it up for Jesus to drink , saying, “Leave Him alone. Let us see if Elijah (my God is YHWH) comes to take Him down.
15.37: But Jesus (YHWH saves) let out a loud cry and breathed His last.
15.38: And the veil of the temple was torn in two from top to bottom.
15.39: When the centurion standing there in front of Jesus saw how [having cried out,] He had breathed His last, he said, “Truly this man was the Son of God {Theou}!
15.40: And there were also women watching from a distance. Among them were Mary (beloved) Magdalene (of Magdala), Mary the mother of James (supplanter / follower) the younger and of Joses (Joseph), and Salome (peace).
15.41: These women had followed Jesus and ministered to Him while He was in Galilee (circuit), and there were many other women who had come up to Jerusalem (city of peace) with Him.
15.42: Now it was already evening. Since it was Preparation Day (that is, the day before the Sabbath),
15.43: Joseph (he will add) of Arimathea (High place), a prominent Council member who himself was waiting for the kingdom of God {Theou}, boldly went to Pilate (armed with a spear) to ask for the body of Jesus (YHWH saves).
15.44: Pilate (armed with a spear) was surprised to hear that Jesus was already dead, so he summoned the centurion to ask if this was so.
15.45: When Pilate had confirmed it with the centurion, he granted the body to Joseph (he will add).
15.46: So Joseph bought a linen cloth, took down the body of Jesus, wrapped it in the cloth, and placed it in a tomb that had been cut out of the rock. Then he rolled a stone against the entrance to the tomb.
15.47: Mary (beloved) Magdalene (of Magdala) and Mary the mother of Joseph (Joses) saw where His body was placed.

16.1: When the Sabbath was over, Mary (beloved) Magdalene (of Magdala), Mary the mother of James (supplanter / follower), and Salome (peace) bought spices so they could go and anoint the body of Jesus.
16.2: Very early on the first day of the week \And very early on the first of the Sabbaths,/, just after sunrise, they went to the tomb.
16.3: They were asking one another, “Who will roll away the stone from the entrance of the tomb?
16.4: But when they looked up, they saw that the stone had been rolled away, even though it was extremely large.
16.5: When they entered the tomb, they saw a young man dressed in a white robe sitting on the right side, and they were alarmed.
16.6: But he said to them, “Do not be alarmed. You? are looking for Jesus (YHWH saves) the Nazarene, who was crucified. He has risen! He is not here! See (behold) the place where they put Him.
16.7: But go, tell His disciples (pupils) and Peter (rock), ‘He is going ahead of you? into Galilee (circuit). There you? will see Him, just as He told you?.
16.8: So the women left the tomb and ran away, trembling and bewildered. And in their fear they did not say a word to anyone.
16.9: Early on the first day of the week, after Jesus had risen, He appeared first to Mary (beloved) Magdalene (of Magdala), from whom He had driven out seven demons.
16.10: She went and told those who had been with Him, who were mourning and weeping.
16.11: And when they heard that Jesus was alive and she had seen Him, they did not believe it.
16.12: After this, Jesus appeared in a different form to two of them as they walked along in the country.
16.13: And they went back and reported it to the rest, but they did not believe (have faith) them either.
16.14: Later, as they were eating, Jesus appeared to the Eleven and rebuked them for their unbelief and hardness of heart, because they did not believe (have faith) those who had seen Him after He had risen.
16.15: And He said to them, “Go into all the world and preach the gospel (good news) to every creature.
16.16: Whoever believes and is baptized (submerged) will be saved, but whoever does not believe will be condemned.
16.17: And these signs will accompany those who believe: In My name they will drive out demons; they will speak in new tongues (languages);
16.18: they will pick up snakes with their hands, and if they drink any deadly poison, it will not harm them; they will lay their hands on the sick, and they will be made well.
16.19: After the Lord {Kyrios} Jesus [After the LORD] had spoken to them, He was taken up into heaven and sat down at the right hand of God {Theou}.
16.20: And they went out and preached everywhere, and the Lord {Kyriou} worked through them, confirming His word by the signs that accompanied it.
